excerpts:
Section Title: India: SEBI & RBI Restrictions on Global Mutual Funds – What FMEs Need to Know
Content:
Author: Veronica Pasumarthy
|
Date: 23 June 2025
|
Categories: Shareholder Disclosures
To manage forex volatility, SEBI and RBI have imposed strict caps on overseas mutual fund investments:
**Industry-wide cap:** USD 7 billion
**AMC-specific cap:** USD 1 billion
**ETF-specific cap:** USD 1 billion

Section Title: PGIM India Mutual Fund restricts subscription in its 3 international funds
Content:
SECTIONS
PGIM India Mutual Fund restricts subscription in its 3 international funds
By
Surbhi Khanna
, ETMarkets.com Last Updated: Mar 10, 2026, 04:44:00 PM IST
Rate Story
[](https://www.google.com/preferences/source?q=economictimes)
[Follow us](https://whatsapp.com/channel/0029Vb6dOI1EKyZPPqpH7I0y)
Share
Font Size
Abc Small
Abc Medium
Abc Large
Save
Print
Comment
Synopsis
PGIM India Mutual Fund is placing temporary restrictions on subscriptions for three international funds. This measure is to prevent exceeding overseas investment limits. Transactions received after March 9, 2026, will not be processed. Existing Systematic Investment Plans will continue. Other transactions like switches and redemptions remain unaffected. This follows previous reopenings and suspensions of subscriptions in these funds.
Section Title: PGIM India Mutual Fund restricts subscription in its 3 international funds
Content:
ETMarkets.com On February 5,2026 the fund house announced reopening of subscription in these three international funds with effect from February 6 with maximum of Rs 5 lakh per day.
[PGIM India Mutual Fund](https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/pgim-india-mutual-fund/mutual_funds_search/amcid-9055.cms) has announced the temporary restrictions for subscription to units in its three international funds - PGIM India Global Equity Opportunities Fund of Fund , PGIM India Emerging Markets Equity Fund of Fund and PGIM India Global Select Real Estate Securities Fund of Fund with effect from March 10.
According to a notice cum addendum issued by the fund house, this move comes in order to avoid breach of the aforesaid overseas limits, following transactions received post the cut off timing of March 09, 2026 shall not be accepted and processed in these three international funds.
Section Title: PGIM India Mutual Fund restricts subscription in its 3 international funds
Content:

The fund house on installments of Systematic Investment / Transfer Plans existing as on March 09, 2026 said that existing SIP / STP installments will continue. There will be no trigger of any switch-out transactions or IDCW transfer out instalments be accepted post the cut off timing of March 09, 2026. However, units may be allotted where the switch out transaction or the systematic/IDCW transfer out leg was processed before the cut off timing of March 09, 2026.
 ...

results.title: Investment Funds 2026 - India  | Global Practice Guides | Chambers and Partners
excerpts:
Section Title: India > Authors > 1. Market Overview > 1.1 State of the Market
Content:
Domestic investment funds regulated by the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI), including privately placed alternative investment funds (“Domestic AIFs”) governed by the SEBI (Alternative Investment Funds) Regulations, 2012 (“AIF Regulations”) and retail mutual funds governed by the SEBI (Mutual Funds) Regulations, 1996 and the SEBI (Mutual Funds) Regulations, 2026, which shall come into effect from 1 April 2026 (“MF Regulations”). Under the AIF Regulations, Domestic AIFs generally take the form of trusts and must have a manager and a sponsor (to maintain continuing interest in them), with both regulated by the SEBI. Investment funds (“GIFT Funds”) set up at International Financial Services Centre (IFSC) Gujarat International Finance Tec-City (GIFT or GIFT City).
 ... 
Section Title: India > Authors > 1. Market Overview > 1.1 State of the Market
Content:
GIFT Funds need not be limited to India-focused funds, and can be global funds raising capital from resident Indians or overseas investors.
Domestically, retail funds are regulated as Mutual Funds, as discussed in 3. Fund Formation. GIFT Funds can also launch schemes for retail investors subject to the criteria specified under the FM Regulations.
 ... 
Section Title: India > Authors > 2. Alternative Investment Funds > 2.2.3 Restrictions on Investors
Content:
Insurance companies: Insurers are permitted to invest in Category I and Category II Domestic AIFs that make investments in specified sectors, and up to 10% of the corpus. The insurers must not invest in Domestic AIFs that invest in securities of companies outside India. RBI regulated entities: Indian banks, non-banking financial companies (NBFCs) and other entities regulated by the RBI (REs) are limited to investing a maximum of 10% of the corpus of a Domestic AIF, and collective contribution by REs in a Domestic AIF should not be more than 20% of the corpus.
 ... 
Section Title: India > Authors > 3. Retail Funds > 3.3.1 Regulatory Regime
Content:
Further, an industry-wide limit of USD7 billion has been set for overseas investments, with a USD1 billion cap per individual mutual fund. In addition, mutual funds are permitted to make overseas investments in an Exchange Traded Fund with an industry-wide limit of USD1 billion and a cap of USD300 million per individual mutual fund.
See **2.3.1 Regulatory Regime** .

Mutual Fund Market News
ICICI Prudential MF resumes three overseas funds
Section Title: ICICI PRUDENTIAL MF RESUMES THREE OVERSEAS FUNDS
Content:
Published on Jan 22, 2026 14:51
ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund said it will resume subscriptions in three of its international schemes from 27 January 2026, while imposing investment caps to manage overseas exposure.
The fund house said fresh and additional investments in ICICI Prudential US Bluechip Equity Fund, ICICI Prudential NASDAQ 100 Index Fund, and ICICI Prudential Strategic Metal and Energy Equity Fund of Fund will be capped at Rs 2 lakh per PAN per month per scheme. The limit will apply across all modes, including lumpsum investments, switches, SIPs and STPs.
Section Title: ICICI PRUDENTIAL MF RESUMES THREE OVERSEAS FUNDS
Content:
For systematic investments, the AMC has set per-PAN limits based on frequency, with daily SIP or STP capped at Rs 10,000, weekly at Rs 50,000, fortnightly SIP at Rs 1 lakh, monthly SIP or STP at Rs 2 lakh, and quarterly SIP or STP at Rs 6 lakh. These limits will apply only to fresh subscriptions or new systematic registrations, while existing SIP and STP instalments will continue without restrictions.
The fund house said it may consider suspending further subscriptions if the available overseas investment limit under any scheme approaches the regulatory cap.
The move follows earlier restrictions imposed after Sebi directed mutual funds to halt fresh overseas investments as the industry-wide overseas investment limit was nearing exhaustion.

International FoF
Global Funds, also known as International Fund of Funds, invest in funds domiciled outside India, which gives investors exposure to companies listed in other geographies. For instance, an Emerging Markets Fund will help you get exposure to a variety of stocks listed in countries such as China, Taiwan, South Korea, among others. Similarly, a Developed Market Fund will give you exposure to U.S, Europe, Canada, Japan and so on. Investing in Global Funds helps you overcome home bias, which is a tendency to over-invest in domestic equities.

Tax planning with mutual funds
Frequently Asked Questions
Expand All
What are Global Funds?
 ... 
Fund of Funds pools money from investors in the source fund (Feeder) which in turn invests the money in the target fund (Master). Fund of Funds can be Domestic or Global. Domestic FoFs invest in domestic equity or debt funds while Global Funds invest in funds operated in other countries.
How to choose a Global Fund?
Global Funds offer a variety of strategies and risks. So you should choose a fund that suits your risk appetite and goal. Ideally, one may consider investing in a diversified strategy that can invest across geographies (emerging and global) and market capitalisation to diversify meaningfully. So each Global Fund is different and investors should understand the investment strategy/objective of the fund.
What is the difference between a global fund and an international fund?
Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) classifies these funds under Fund of Fund (Overseas). They are also known as Global Funds or International Funds.
